data.narrative:
eleQtron is a Series A quantum computing company founded by Jan Leisse that has raised €50M+ to date. The quantum computing sector comprises a fragmented peer universe of 11 companies across public and private markets: 3 publicly traded peers (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS) and 8 private companies including IonQ, Quantinuum, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum, and Infleqtion.

Standard peer-median valuation methodology is not applicable here. The quantum computing sector lacks sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables meeting standard screening thresholds—only 0 same-stage peers, 1 adjacent-stage peer, and 0 similar-tier peers were identified. The narrow-set median approach requires a minimum of 3 datapoints to be reliable; this universe falls short. A broad all-sector median would introduce material selection bias given the heterogeneity of business models, maturity stages, and technical approaches across the 7 same-sector peers.

Without a sufficiently dense comparable set, traditional triangulation methods cannot be applied with confidence. Valuation of eleQtron would require alternative approaches—such as DCF analysis anchored to hardware commercialization timelines, or staged venture method calibrated to quantum computing development milestones—neither of which are supported by the data provided. The key uncertainty remains the lack of recent Series A transactions in quantum computing that would establish stage-appropriate pricing precedent.
